title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Synchronous HTTP blocks R session waiting reply. Asynchronous mode lets fire multiple requests process results arrive — useful bots poll several endpoints place orders parallel.

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Event loop: R built-event loop like Node.js Python’s asyncio. Promises resolve event loop ticks via later::run_now(). scripts vignettes, drain loop (!loop_empty()) run_now(). Shiny apps event loop runs automatically.

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: coro package provides async() await() writing async code reads like synchronous code:
code: library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- async(function() {   # These run concurrently   bars_promise <- md$get_bars(     symbol = "AAPL",     timeframe = "1Day",     start = "2024-01-01",     end = "2024-01-31"   )   clock_promise <- md$get_clock()    bars <- await(bars_promise)   clock <- await(clock_promise)    cat("Got", nrow(bars), "bars for AAPL\n")   cat("Market is", if (clock$is_open) "OPEN" else "CLOSED", "\n")   return(invisible(NULL)) })  main() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Fire multiple requests simultaneously collect results:
code: library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE) symbols <- c("AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL", "AMZN", "TSLA")  # Launch all requests in parallel, then await each in sequence inside # a single async() block. The requests run concurrently — only the # awaits are sequential. collect <- async(function() {   bar_promises <- lapply(symbols, function(sym) {     return(md$get_latest_bar(symbol = sym))   })   results <- vector("list", length(bar_promises))   for (i in seq_along(bar_promises)) {     results[[i]] <- await(bar_promises[[i]])   }   return(data.table::rbindlist(results, fill = TRUE)) })  result_promise <- collect() while (!later::loop_empty()) {   later::run_now() } print(await(result_promise))

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Place orders asynchronously monitor status:
code:
library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  trading <- AlpacaTrading$new(async = TRUE) acct <- AlpacaAccount$new(async = TRUE)  main <- async(function() {   # Place order   order <- await(trading$add_order(     symbol = "AAPL",     side = "buy",     type = "limit",     time_in_force = "day",     qty = 1,     limit_price = 150   ))   cat("Order placed:", order$id, "
")    # Check account   info <- await(acct$get_account())   cat("Buying power:", info$buying_power, "
")    # Check positions   positions <- await(acct$get_positions())   cat("Open positions:", nrow(positions), "
")   return(invisible(NULL)) })  main() while (!loop_empty()) {   run_now() }

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Shiny, event loop runs automatically. Just return promises reactive expressions:
code: library(shiny) library(alpaca)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   bars <- reactive({     return(md$get_bars(       symbol = input$symbol,       timeframe = "1Day",       start = input$start,       end = input$end     ))   })    output$chart <- renderPlot({     coro::async(function() {       data <- await(bars())       return(plot(         data$t,         data$c,         type = "l",         main = paste(input$symbol, "Close Price"),         xlab = "Date",         ylab = "Price"       ))     })()   })   return(invisible(NULL)) }

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Use tryCatch() inside async() block — await() re-throws promise rejection normal R error, JS try/catch pattern works directly:
code: library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- async(function() {   return(tryCatch(     {       data <- await(md$get_bars(         symbol = "INVALID_SYMBOL",         timeframe = "1Day"       ))       cat("Got", nrow(data), "bars\n")     },     error = function(err) {       cat("Error:", conditionMessage(err), "\n")       return(invisible(NULL))     }   )) })  main() while (!later::loop_empty()) {   later::run_now() }

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Four R6 classes plus handful standalone helpers wrap Alpaca REST API. Every class supports synchronous asynchronous (promise-based) operation via httr2. Standalone (class):

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Set credentials .Renviron: R:
code: ALPACA_API_KEY=your-api-key ALPACA_API_SECRET=your-api-secret ALPACA_API_ENDPOINT=https://paper-api.alpaca.markets   # paper trading library(alpaca) keys   <- get_api_keys() market <- AlpacaMarketData$new(keys = keys) acct   <- AlpacaAccount$new(keys = keys) trading <- AlpacaTrading$new(keys = keys) opts   <- AlpacaOptions$new(keys = keys)

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: methods auto-paginate: follow Alpaca’s next_page_token across pages return full start..end range one data.table, rather single ~1,000-row page API returns default. (get_bars_multi() 1,000-row cap total budget across requested symbols, filled alphabetically — without pagination later symbols silently came back empty.) Tune limit (page size, default 10000), sleep (-page throttle seconds, default 0.3), max_pages (runaway guard, default 1000; pass Inf unbounded). max_pages hit data remains, partial result returned warning — never silently truncated.

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: Now ’ve seen surface, rules used decide row returned data.table represents. Every method follows one principle: Identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity. trade row, article row, asset row. Anything nested entity becomes either flat column row additional row different axis — never list column. exact treatment depends nested shape. four.

what: Treatment A — ; collapse for arrays of strings
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: entity variable-length array plain strings (tags, codes, permissions, identifiers), join one character column ; separator. Filter grepl list assets: Recover original character vector splitting: separator ; rather , semicolons far less likely appear inside one values . fields value legally contain ; — currently just news image URLs, ; valid URL sub-delimiter — value double-encoded joining (first % → %25, ; → %3B), single URLdecode() splitting recovers original string losslessly, even source URL already contained percent-escapes %3B: Parallel arrays (e.g. news image size url) collapsed independently separate columns. entries per-element field others omit , missing values appear empty tokens, literal "NA": ’s used: attributes assets, conditions trades quotes (single, multi, historical variants), symbols / image_sizes / image_urls news.
code: asset <- market$get_asset("AAPL") asset$attributes #> "fractional_eh_enabled;has_options;overnight_tradable" assets <- market$get_assets() options_assets <- assets[grepl("has_options", attributes)] strsplit(asset$attributes, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] #> [1] "fractional_eh_enabled" "has_options" "overnight_tradable" news <- market$get_news(symbols = "AAPL", limit = 1) urls  <- strsplit(news$image_urls[1], ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] urls  <- vapply(urls, URLdecode, character(1)) news[id == 12350, image_sizes] #> "large;"          # second image had no `size`; never "large;NA"

what: Treatment B — Long format for arrays of objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: array elements records (price, size, symbol, …), explode one row per element parent fields replicated. position-index column added order matters (level, leg_index). ’s used: bids / asks levels crypto orderbook; assets watchlists (one row per (watchlist, asset) asset_ prefixed columns).
code: ob <- market$get_crypto_orderbook("BTC/USD") ob #>     symbol side level    price  size           timestamp #>     <char> <char> <int>   <num> <num>              <POSc> #>  1: BTC/USD  bid     1 42950.50  0.5 2024-01-15 20:00:00 #>  2: BTC/USD  bid     2 42949.00  1.2 2024-01-15 20:00:00 #>  3: BTC/USD  ask     1 42951.00  0.3 2024-01-15 20:00:00 #>  4: BTC/USD  ask     2 42952.50  0.8 2024-01-15 20:00:00

what: Treatment B′ — Parent + leg rows for multi-leg orders
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: variant Treatment B applies multi-leg orders (bracket, oco, oto, mleg). parent leg get row single flat table; two helper columns disambiguate: leg_index (integer): NA parent row, 1, 2, … leg. parent_order_id (character): NA parent row, parent’s id leg row. simple order returns single row helper columns NA. ’s used: add_order / get_order / get_orders / get_order_by_client_id / modify_order AlpacaTrading.
code: # A bracket order: 1 parent + 2 legs = 3 rows o <- trading$add_order(   symbol = "AAPL", side = "buy", type = "limit",   time_in_force = "gtc", qty = 1, limit_price = 1,   order_class = "bracket",   take_profit = list(limit_price = 500),   stop_loss   = list(stop_price = 0.5, limit_price = 0.4) ) nrow(o)                                                   # 3 o[is.na(parent_order_id), .(id, side)]                    # the parent parent_id <- o[is.na(parent_order_id), id] o[parent_order_id == parent_id,   .(leg_index, side, limit_price, stop_price)]            # the two legs

what: Treatment C — Wide prefix for fixed-schema nested objects
title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: nested data object (array) known fixed key set, flatten inner fields parent_child columns row. parent stays one row. ’s used: admin_configurations_* / user_configurations_* get_account; latest_trade_* / latest_quote_* / minute_bar_* / daily_bar_* / prev_daily_bar_* get_snapshot multi-symbol variant.
code: acct <- acct$get_account() acct$admin_configurations_max_options_trading_level   # was nested under admin_configurations #> 2 acct$user_configurations_dtbp_check                   # was nested under user_configurations #> "both"

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: get_snapshot() Treatment C one inner Treatment . top-level object wide-flattened latest_trade_* / latest_quote_* / *_bar_* columns; inner c arrays trade quote sections become semicolon-joined latest_trade_conditions / latest_quote_conditions columns. c field bar sections close price (scalar number), ’s touched — ends numeric *_close column.
code: snap <- market$get_snapshot("AAPL") snap$latest_trade_conditions   # "@;T"          — Treatment A inside the C flatten snap$latest_quote_conditions   # "R"            — same snap$minute_bar_close          # 304.7          — left alone, numeric

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: apply every method, regardless treatment. Empty arrays become NA, list cells. asset attributes, trade condition codes, article images — set relevant column NA_character_. Downstream code .na() keeps working; list-column gymnastics. Empty responses return empty data.table. synthetic stub rows. nrow() honest count “things got back.”

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: considered didn’t end needing list two data.tables — returning e.g. list(orders = ..., legs = ...) keyed foreign key genuinely heterogeneous nested arrays. Every nested field across package fits one four treatments . escape hatch held reserve future endpoint forces .

title: Package Tour and Data-Shape Conventions
text: convention applied sibling binance kucoin packages. trader hops can rely : canonical column names (symbol, timestamp, price, size, side, conditions, …), row-shape contract (entity-per-row, never list column), recovery idioms (grepl, strsplit, URLdecode, .na(parent_order_id) filter multi-leg orders). predictability makes connector layer (exchanges package, top tradebot-core) easy write: raw wrapper returns data connector can re-shape without special-case list columns wrap--unwrap nested structures.

title: API Wrapper to Alpaca Trading Platform
text: API responses returned data.table objects three transformations applied: snake_case column names – camelCase keys JSON response (e.g. latestTrade, nextClose) converted snake_case (latest_trade, next_close). handful endpoints additionally expand abbreviated single-letter keys returned Alpaca’s bar/trade/quote feeds (e.g. t → datetime bar’s reference time t → timestamp trade/quote event time, p → price, o/h/l/c → open/high/low/close, vw → volume_weighted_avg_price) resulting column names self-describing. full mapping per endpoint documented method-level @return blocks. RFC-3339 timestamps POSIXct – Every timestamp Alpaca API emits RFC-3339 form parsed , regardless endpoint: bars, trades, quotes, snapshots (incl. five nested *_timestamp fields), orderbooks, news (created_at, updated_at), orders (created_at, updated_at, submitted_at, filled_at, expired_at, canceled_at, failed_at, replaced_at), account created_at, watchlist created_at / updated_at, activities transaction_time. POSIXct columns displayed UTC default. See method’s @return per-column types. One entity = one row, list columns – every endpoint normalised shape policy (see Data-shape conventions ) returned data.table contains list columns. small number legacy endpoints go generic parser may still surface nested arrays list columns; called individually @return. fields dropped normalising parsers (parse_trades, parse_quotes, parse_news, parse_snapshot, parse_asset/parse_assets, parse_order/parse_orders, parse_watchlist). Every scalar array field present Alpaca response surfaces column resulting data.table – collapsed, exploded, wide-prefixed per shape policy. don’t need column, drop .

title: API Wrapper to Alpaca Trading Platform
text: Alpaca’s JSON responses sometimes contain nested arrays objects. keep returns intuitive (consistent across alpaca / binance / kucoin packages), every method follows one rule: identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity. four cases:

title: API Wrapper to Alpaca Trading Platform
text: Filter grepl: Split back original character vector: URL-encoded fields (currently just news image_urls, Alpaca legitimately uses ; inside URLs) round-trip via URLdecode(). encoding lossless directions: URL double-encoded (% → %25, ; → %3B) joining, single URLdecode() splitting recovers original string even upstream URL already contained %3B percent-escapes: parallel arrays like image_sizes / image_urls, one element omits per-image field missing value becomes empty token (e.g. "large;"), never literal string "NA" — real "NA" value remains unambiguous missing one.
code: # All assets that support options trading options_assets <- assets[grepl("has_options", attributes)]  # All trades reported on the consolidated tape trades_on_tape <- trades[grepl("T", conditions)] strsplit(dt$attributes[1], ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] # [1] "fractional_eh_enabled" "has_options" "overnight_tradable" urls <- strsplit(news$image_urls[1], ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]] urls <- vapply(urls, URLdecode, character(1))

title: API Wrapper to Alpaca Trading Platform
text: Multi-leg orders return flat data.table one row per “order” (parent legs treated equally). Two helper columns disambiguate: leg_index (integer) – NA parent row; 1, 2, ... leg. parent_order_id (character) – NA parent row; parent’s id leg.
code: # Just the parent orders (filter out legs) dt[is.na(parent_order_id)]  # All legs of one specific bracket dt[parent_order_id == "<parent-uuid>"]

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: Provides methods retrieving account information, managing positions, viewing portfolio history, querying account activities Alpaca's Trading API. Inherits AlpacaBase. methods support synchronous asynchronous execution depending async parameter construction.

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: Account: Retrieve current account details (equity, buying power, etc.). Positions: View open positions specific position symbol. Close Positions: Close individual positions (fully partially). Options Exercise: Exercise options positions. Account Config: View update account configurations. Portfolio History: View historical portfolio value snapshots. Activities: Query account activity history (fills, dividends, etc.). Watchlists: Create, update, manage symbol watchlists.

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: method auto-paginate. walk every activity matches filters, pass last row's id back page_token; stop returned page shorter page_size (.e. reached tail). worked example:   Automated pagination (drop page_size, add n / max_total) planned follow-release; method's public API remain backward-compatible.
code: library(data.table) acct <- AlpacaAccount$new()  pages <- list() token <- NULL repeat {   dt <- acct$get_activities(     activity_types = "FILL",     direction = "desc",     page_size = 100L,        # the hard server-side cap     page_token = token   )   if (nrow(dt) == 0L) break   pages[[length(pages) + 1L]] <- dt   if (nrow(dt) < 100L) break # short page == last page   token <- tail(dt$id, 1L) } all_fills <- rbindlist(pages, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)

text: period (scalar<character> | NULL) time period history. Examples: "1D", "1W", "1M", "3M", "1A", "". Mutually exclusive providing start end. timeframe (scalar<character> | NULL) resolution time series: "1Min", "5Min", "15Min", "1H", "1D". start (scalar<character> | NULL) start timestamp RFC3339 format. Defaults end minus period. end (scalar<character> | NULL) end timestamp RFC3339 format. intraday_reporting (scalar<character> | NULL) "market_hours" (default), "extended_hours", "continuous" (24/7 crypto charts). pnl_reset (scalar<character> | NULL) "per_day" (default) "no_reset". Set "no_reset" continuous crypto PnL. date_start (scalar<character> | NULL) deprecated alias start. Earlier releases used name silently ignored API. Now forwarded start deprecation warning. removed future release. date_end (scalar<character> | NULL) deprecated alias end. notes date_start.

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: activity_types (scalar<character> | NULL) comma-separated activity types filter (e.g., "FILL", "DIV", "TRANS"). See Alpaca docs full list. Mutually exclusive category. date (scalar<character> | NULL) filter specific date ("YYYY-MM-DD"). (scalar<character> | NULL) activities timestamp. (scalar<character> | NULL) activities timestamp. direction (scalar<character> | NULL) "asc" "desc". page_size (scalar<count [1, 101[> | NULL) max results per page. Alpaca caps 100 /v2/account/activities; values 100 return HTTP 422 server-side. method validates cap -front abort()s clear message rather letting vendor error leak . Must single non-NA integerish value provided. Default NULL lets Alpaca pick server-side default (currently 100). page_token (scalar<character> | NULL) cursor next page. activities id last row previous page (Alpaca's activity IDs sortable cursors, opaque tokens). See "Pagination" section . category (scalar<character> | NULL) broad category filter: "trade_activity" "non_trade_activity". Mutually exclusive activity_types.

text: (Activity | promise<Activity>) activities. Columns beyond guaranteed Activity set vary activity type. id per-activity cursor used paging (see "Pagination"). empty response returns zero-row table carrying Activity columns.

title: AlpacaBase: Abstract Base Class for Alpaca API Clients — AlpacaBase
text: Provides shared infrastructure Alpaca R6 classes, including API credential management, sync/async execution mode, standardised request funnel. Inherits transport connectcore::RestClient customises two venue-specific seams: .sign() adds Alpaca's API-key headers, .parse_envelope() reads Alpaca's error shape. Every endpoint method subclass delegates inherited private$.request().

title: AlpacaBase: Abstract Base Class for Alpaca API Clients — AlpacaBase
text: async parameter controls execution mode API methods: async = FALSE (default): methods return results directly (data.table, etc.). async = TRUE: methods return promises::promise objects resolve types. async, use coro::async() await() promises::() consume results. promises package must installed async mode (Suggests dependency).

title: AlpacaBase: Abstract Base Class for Alpaca API Clients — AlpacaBase
text: Alpaca uses header-based authentication two headers: APCA-API-KEY-ID: API key ID APCA-API-SECRET-KEY: API secret key HMAC signing required — credentials sent directly headers, exactly .sign() override .

title: AlpacaBase: Abstract Base Class for Alpaca API Clients — AlpacaBase
text: max_tries > 1 opts every GET client makes — single requests auto-paginated reads (e.g. historical bars) alike — automatic retry transient failure (HTTP 408/429/5xx dropped connection) jittered backoff, delegated connectcore::build_request(). Retry hard GET-carve-: non-idempotent verb (order POST, cancel DELETE) never auto-retried, resend can never double-submit order. Leave default 1 live trading — trader layer single retry authority (routes typed error class manages cooldowns); raise research backfill reads.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: Provides methods retrieving market data Alpaca's REST API, including historical bars (OHLCV), latest quotes/trades, snapshots, asset info, market calendar, clock. Inherits AlpacaBase. methods support synchronous asynchronous execution depending async parameter construction.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: Bars: Retrieve historical OHLCV candlestick data stocks. Trades: Access historical latest trade data. Quotes: Access historical latest quote (NBBO) data. Snapshots: Get real-time snapshot symbol's latest state. Assets: Query available tradeable assets metadata. Calendar: Get market open/close schedule. Clock: Check current market status (open/closed). Corporate Actions: Query dividends, splits, mergers, spinoffs. News: Retrieve market news articles filtered symbol/date. Screener: active stocks, top market movers.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: client wraps Alpaca's /v2/ market-data endpoints, US-. returned date / time columns coerced Date POSIXct ergonomic use: Endpoints whose JSON carries explicit RFC-3339 offset (bars, trades, quotes, news, snapshots, clock) parsed exact instant. Clock displayed America/New_York; bars / trades / quotes / news UTC. Use lubridate::with_tz() view timezone. calendar endpoint returns naive wall-clock times ("09:30", "0400") offset payload. Alpaca explicitly document timezone calendar reference page, values Eastern Time inference (US-venues, 09:30 matches NYSE/NASDAQ open, every SDK treats ET, market-data FAQ confirms NY tz bar aggregation). localise named tz America/New_York, DST transitions flip automatically. assumption safe /v2/. Alpaca's /v3/ multi-market endpoints adopted, per-market timezone lookup replace single hard-coded tz. constant lives R/helpers_parse.R (search TODO(v3)).

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: symbol (scalar<character>) ticker symbol (e.g., "AAPL"). timeframe (scalar<character>) bar timeframe. Valid values: "1Min" "59Min", "1Hour" "23Hour", "1Day", "1Week", "1Month" "12Month". start (scalar<character> | NULL) start date/time (RFC-3339 "YYYY-MM-DD"). end (scalar<character> | NULL) end date/time. limit (scalar<count [1, 10001[> | NULL) max bars per page (1-10000, default 10000). method auto-paginates via next_page_token, full start..end range returned regardless limit; sets page size (thus number requests). adjustment (scalar<character> | NULL) price adjustment type. One comma-separated combination : "raw", "split", "dividend", "spin-", "". Default "raw". feed (scalar<character> | NULL) data feed source: "sip" (default, US exchanges), "iex", "otc", "boats". sort (scalar<character> | NULL) "asc" (default) "desc". page_token (scalar<character> | NULL) starting cursor. Normally left NULL — auto-pagination begins start follows cursor . asof (scalar<character> | NULL) -date ("YYYY-MM-DD") used identify underlying entity symbols renamed (e.g. FB -> META). Pass "-" skip symbol mapping. currency (scalar<character> | NULL) ISO 4217 currency returned prices. Default "USD". max_pages (scalar<numeric [1, Inf]> | scalar<integer [1, Inf[>) cap pages fetched (runaway guard). Default 1000 — high enough real requests complete, low enough bound fat-fingered pull. Pass Inf unbounded. hit data remains, partial result returned warning(). sleep (scalar<numeric [0, Inf[>) seconds pause page requests (rate-limit throttle; sync ). Default 0.3.

text: types (character | NULL) corporate-action types return (sent venue's types query param). Valid values: "forward_split", "reverse_split", "unit_split", "stock_dividend", "cash_dividend", "spin_off", "cash_merger", "stock_merger", "stock_and_cash_merger", "redemption", "name_change", "worthless_removal", "rights_distribution", "contract_adjustment", "partial_call", "reorganization". NULL (default) returns every type. Invalid values abort request. symbols (character | NULL) filter ticker symbol(s). NULL (default) returns symbols. start (scalar<character> | NULL) start date ("YYYY-MM-DD"). archive begins 2016. NULL uses venue default. end (scalar<character> | NULL) end date ("YYYY-MM-DD"). NULL uses venue default. limit (scalar<count [1, 1001[> | NULL) max results per page (1-1000, default 1000). method auto-paginates via next_page_token, full range returned regardless limit. sort (scalar<character> | NULL) "asc" (default server-side) "desc". page_token (scalar<character> | NULL) starting cursor. Normally left NULL — auto-pagination begins start follows cursor . max_pages (scalar<numeric [1, Inf]> | scalar<integer [1, Inf[>) cap pages fetched (runaway guard). Default 1000; pass Inf unbounded. hit data remains, partial result returned warning(). sleep (scalar<numeric [0, Inf[>) seconds pause page requests (rate-limit throttle; sync ). Default 0.3.

text: symbol (scalar<character> | NULL) ticker symbol (e.g., "AAPL"). Required order classes except "mleg" (multi-leg options), leg carries symbol. side (scalar<character> | NULL) "buy" "sell". Required order classes except "mleg", leg carries side. type (scalar<character>) order type: "market", "limit", "stop", "stop_limit", "trailing_stop". time_in_force (scalar<character>) "day", "gtc", "opg", "cls", "ioc", "fok". qty (scalar<numeric> | NULL) number shares. Mutually exclusive notional. notional (scalar<numeric> | NULL) dollar amount. Market/day orders . limit_price (scalar<numeric> | NULL) limit price. Required "limit" "stop_limit" orders. stop_price (scalar<numeric> | NULL) stop trigger price. Required "stop" "stop_limit" orders. trail_price (scalar<numeric> | NULL) trailing stop dollar offset. trail_percent (scalar<numeric> | NULL) trailing stop percentage offset. extended_hours (scalar<logical> | NULL) allow pre/post market execution. client_order_id (scalar<character> | NULL) unique client order ID (max 128 chars). order_class (scalar<character> | NULL) "simple", "bracket", "oco", "oto". take_profit (list | NULL) list(limit_price = ...) bracket orders. stop_loss (list | NULL) list(stop_price = ..., limit_price = ...) bracket orders. position_intent (scalar<character> | NULL) "buy_to_open", "buy_to_close", "sell_to_open", "sell_to_close". legs (list | NULL) leg objects (max 4) multi-leg options strategies. Required order_class = "mleg". advanced_instructions (list | NULL) routing instructions Alpaca Elite Smart Router.

title: AlpacaTrading: Order Management — AlpacaTrading
text: (Order | promise<Order>) order(s). simple order returns single row. bracket / oco / oto / mleg order returns parent row plus one row per leg — see "Multi-leg orders" section README vignette("data-shapes") parent + leg layout. Columns include id, client_order_id, symbol, side, type, time_in_force, status, qty, filled_qty, filled_avg_price, limit_price stop_price (character); order timestamps created_at, updated_at, submitted_at, filled_at, expired_at, canceled_at, failed_at replaced_at (POSIXct, UTC) present; leg_index (integer, NA parent row, 1..N leg); parent_order_id (character, NA parent row, parent's id leg). Use dt[.na(parent_order_id)] keep just parent rows, dt[parent_order_id == "<uuid>"] legs one bracket.

title: Alpaca return shapes — alpaca_shapes
text: Reusable roxyassert @type shapes data.tables returned Alpaca R6 client methods (AlpacaMarketData, AlpacaTrading, AlpacaAccount, AlpacaOptions). public method documents return Shape | promise<Shape>; contract roclet expands shape generated assert_return_* helper, checks every listed column present column type. assert_has_columns requires listed columns tolerates EXTRA ones, shape names columns Alpaca guarantees every response flows contract; venue-optional columns (e.g. flattened admin_configurations_* account fields, options greeks_* / implied_volatility columns, richer single-order price fields) ride along un-asserted extras. Column-type conventions, matched parsers actually emit (verified tests/testthat mock fixtures): character string-typed price/quantity fields Alpaca returns JSON strings (API never narrows numbers, neither ); integer bar volume / trade_count trade/quote size fields; numeric (strict double) true floating-point prices; POSIXct parsed timestamps Date calendar dates. column marked | NA value can legitimately missing present row. Alpaca encodes whole-number price without decimal point, raw JSON parser realises bar/trade/ quote price integer; parsers coerce every price/vwap column clean numeric double .numeric(), column stable numeric type (nullability set separately, per API's optional-field contract: measurement / venue-optional columns | NA, structural columns stay strict, following dereckscompany/.github discussion #2). @genassert omitted: generated assert_type_<Shape>() called internally, leaf connector nothing downstream consumes , shapes expand inline methods' assert_return_* contracts .

text: New method wrapping GET /v1/corporate-actions Market Data host (data.alpaca.markets) — Alpaca’s historical corporate-actions dataset (splits, dividends, mergers, spin-offs, name changes, redemptions, ) reaching back 2016, source equities backtest needs reconstruct split/dividend adjustments. distinct pre-existing get_corporate_actions(), wraps Trading API’s /v2/corporate_actions/announcements recent-events feed (marked DEPRECATED Alpaca); kept, difference documented method. tradebot-data-scraper alpaca-rest collector previously call endpoint thin client wrapper expose . endpoint returns records grouped action type, type carrying different field subset. Following house rule heterogeneous rows, method stacks one data.table type discriminator (venue’s plural group key, e.g. "cash_dividends", "forward_splits") reindexes single canonical schema, narrow types query still returns full, stably-typed column set. Types follow NA-audit conventions: id type strict character; symbol/CUSIP fields (symbol, acquirer_symbol, new_cusip, …) character | NA; rate/cash fields (rate, old_rate, new_rate, cash_rate, …) numeric | NA; every date field (ex_date, payable_date, effective_date, …) Date | NA (venue-optional per action type, matching earlier corporate-actions NA audit); foreign/special flags logical | NA. Auto-paginates via next_page_token (mirroring get_bars_multi()), works synchronous asynchronous (promise) modes. Grounded live endpoint 2026-07-14 (AAPL 4:1 TSLA 5:1 2020 splits, full 16-type enum). empty range returns typed zero-row table via new empty_dt_corporate_actions_history() constructor.

what: Opt-in request retry at construction (max_tries), a hard GET-only carve-out (closes #19)
title: alpaca 0.9.0
text: Every client class constructor (via AlpacaBase) gains max_tries argument (scalar<integer [1, 10]>, default 1 = retry) threaded connectcore’s retry machinery. Setting 1 opts every GET client makes — single requests auto-paginated reads (e.g. historical bars) alike — automatic retry transient failure (HTTP 408/429/5xx dropped connection) jittered backoff. Retry hard GET-carve-: non-idempotent verb (order POST, cancel DELETE) never auto-retried, resend can never double-submit order. default 1 leaves live-trading behaviour unchanged — trader layer stays single retry authority ; raise max_tries research backfill reads. resolves resilience question raised #19: resilience exposed construction reaches every request single alpaca_build_request() funnel (alpaca_paginate() alpaca_build_request() gain max_tries argument paginated bar backfills honour ). Requires connectcore (>= 0.5.0), GET-guard enforced one shared request funnel.

what: Column-type NA audit (org discussion #2): measurement columns tolerate legitimately-missing venue values
title: alpaca 0.8.0
text: per-connector type-fidelity audit reaches alpaca. Thirty-five columns across nine return shapes move strict | NA, grounded alpaca-py SDK’s Optional markings — venue’s authoritative statement fields can absent normal response. headline fixes ones crashed live loop: Position rows volatile markets (market_value, unrealized_pl friends go null Alpaca price symbol), multi-leg parent Orders (symbol/side/type null parent row), notional orders (qty null), CorporateAction date fields absent per action type. Structural identity columns stay strict. Every loosened string column gains parser coercion venue null arrives typed NA rather bare logical (known assert-crash trap), via new parse_account/parse_positions/parse_position helpers matching sibling parse_* pattern. Adds test-na-audit.R (+96 assertions).

title: alpaca 0.7.0

Catch alpaca_validation_error input bugs specifically, alpaca_error non-transport Alpaca failure. message strings byte-identical bare rlang::abort() calls replaced (reverse-substitution proves 34 reproduce master exactly; golden tests pin two representative sites), existing tests downstream message greps keep matching. classes purely additive; conditionMessage() inherits(e, "error") unchanged. behaviour changes. Follows org convention (dereckscompany/tradebot-core#30; discussion “throw typed errors, bare strings”). transport/API funnel (abort_alpaca_error, rooted connectcore_error) untouched.

title: alpaca 0.6.0

Several return contracts longer reject legitimately-missing fields, found running live integration tests real API: activity symbol / side / qty / price / transaction_time (non-trade row — fee, journal, dividend — carries none trade columns), option snapshot/chain latest_trade_timestamp / latest_trade_price / latest_trade_size (illiquid option last trade), option-contract open_interest / close_price. now typed | NA method returns row instead crashing.

title: alpaca 0.4.1
text: 64-bit identifiers counters typed numeric coerced parser. trade id, news article id, bar volume / trade_count can exceed 32-bit integer ceiling liquid name. jsonlite already realises JSON integer >= 2^31 double, assert_integer rejects double — integer contract reject parser’s output real response. now typed numeric, coerced .numeric() parser (matching existing MostActives treatment), empty_dt_*() constructor emits numeric(0). Parser-emitted NAs now reflected contract. PortfolioHistory equity / profit_loss / profit_loss_pct numeric | NA (parser’s nums() maps JSON null -data point NA), quote / snapshot ask_price / bid_price (latest_quote_ask_price / latest_quote_bid_price) numeric | NA (illiquid, one-sided book omits side, parser coerces NA). Previously strict-numeric contracts reject genuine response. Non-empty-string guards every required URL-path identifier. method interpolates required id request path — order_id, client_order_id, symbol, symbol_or_id, watchlist_id, underlying_symbol, activity_type, watchlist name — now asserts assert::assert_nonempty_strings() entry, empty string fails fast boundary rather building malformed URL. Typed return shapes confirmation / single-order methods. cancel_order, exercise_option, cancel_watchlist now declare fixed CancelOrderAck / ExerciseAck / CancelWatchlistAck shapes (instead bare data.table), close_position declares OrderCore shape, assert_return_* checks actual columns types. Concrete argument types time helpers. time_convert_from_alpaca() / time_convert_to_alpaca() replace lazy ? parameter type concrete unions (character | logical | NA | NULL POSIXct | character | logical | NA | NULL). Documentation / packaging hygiene. Closed [1, N] page/limit intervals written half-open ([1, N+1[) longer resolve broken roxygen links document() time (validation unchanged — upper bound stays inclusive). .lintr restores object_name_linter (snake_case / SNAKE_CASE / CamelCase) targeted # nolint two simplifyVector arguments mirror httr2::resp_body_json(). DESCRIPTION strips @ref pins Remotes (source-) carries version constraints Imports / Suggests instead. NEWS.md de-wrapped one continuous line per bullet.

title: alpaca 0.4.0
text: Runtime contract enforcement via roxyassert. Every @param @return across package now typed roxyassert’s annotation grammar (prose types remain), generated guards wired : function R6 method validates inputs assert_args_<fn>() entry result assert_return_<fn>() (R6 forms assert_args_<Class>__<method>() / assert_return_<Class>__<method>()). Documented contracts runtime validation now come one source — R/contracts-generated.R, regenerated document() like NAMESPACE. change adds validation : public signature behaviour changes valid input. sync--async methods return validator applied resolved value connectcore::then_or_now(), runs modes (per roxyassert’s promise model). assert now Imports dependency; roxyassert used document() time . Typed return shapes every table-returning method. return contract endpoint method now references reusable @type shape declared R/types_alpaca.R (Bars, BarsMulti, Trade/TradesMulti, Quote/QuotesMulti, Snapshot/SnapshotMulti, Asset, Calendar, Clock, CorporateAction, News, CryptoOrderbook, MostActives, Movers, Contract, Account, AccountConfig, Position, Activity, PortfolioHistory, Watchlists/Watchlist, OrderCore/Order, options OptionTradesMulti/OptionQuotesMulti), assert_return_* now checks actual columns types — merely “data.table”. list-returning endpoints route empty branch typed empty_dt_*() constructors, empty Alpaca response still carries shape’s columns. Two shape refinements match Alpaca’s wire encoding: bar OHLC / vwap trade/quote price columns coerced clean numeric double parser (Alpaca drops decimal point whole-number price, raw JSON realises integer; coercing keeps column type stable contract can stay strict numeric), Watchlist asset_* columns nullable (empty watchlist returns one -NA asset row). Public behaviour valid response unchanged. Strict numeric contracts leaf-connector hygiene. price columns (open/high/low/close/vwap, trade/quote price, snapshot latest_*_price columns) now typed strict numeric rather integer | numeric: parsers coerce .numeric() column always double. MostActives volume / trade_count typed count coerced numeric, large volume overflow integer. two mis-named typed-empty-vector helpers (empty_dt_empty_posixct() / empty_dt_empty_date()) dropped favour inline length-0 lubridate::as_datetime() / as_date() inside empty_dt_* builders. leaf connector downstream consumer, shapes longer export assert_type_* validators (@genassert / @exportassert removed); @type shapes still expand inline method’s assert_return_* contract.

title: alpaca 0.4.0
text: Transport migrated onto connectcore. Alpaca clients now build connectcore’s shared transport base instead private copy. AlpacaBase inherits connectcore::RestClient customises two venue-specific seams: .sign() adds Alpaca’s APCA-API-KEY-ID / APCA-API-SECRET-KEY headers (Alpaca authenticates plain API-key headers — request signing), .parse_envelope() reads Alpaca’s error shape (204 Content, message / msg body fields). Every endpoint method still routes inherited private$.request() funnel, retry, throttle, timeout, sync/async branch now come one shared place. Duplicated transport generic helpers deleted. package’s then_or_now() request-funnel internals gone, replaced connectcore::build_request() / connectcore::then_or_now(). generic JSON->data.table helpers (to_snake_case(), as_dt_row(), as_dt_list(), collapse_string_array_fields()) now imported connectcore; Alpaca-specific parsers time coercions unchanged. Public API, return shapes, behaviour unchanged. alpaca_build_request() alpaca_paginate() keep signatures (now thin wrappers shared funnel), every existing test continues pass. one endpoint relied simplifyVector = TRUE (portfolio history) now coerces parallel arrays , preserving null -> NA alignment. Request bodies byte-identical pre-migration transport. Bodies pre-serialised alpaca_serialize_body() (exact options old httr2::req_body_json() funnel used — auto_unbox = TRUE, digits = 22, null = "null") sent verbatim connectcore’s body_format = "raw" path, wire bytes orders every POST/PUT/PATCH match previous release exactly. single deliberate departure also fixes long-standing bug: one-symbol watchlist now serialises symbols JSON array (["AAPL"]) instead bare scalar ("AAPL"), Alpaca’s watchlist endpoints require. New tests pin body bytes.

title: alpaca 0.3.0
text: get_bars() get_bars_multi() now auto-paginate, returning full start..end range instead single truncated page. Previously issued one request dropped Alpaca’s next_page_token, request whose result exceeded page size silently cut . get_bars_multi() especially damaging: Alpaca applies page limit total row budget across requested symbols (filling symbols alphabetically), single call 9 symbols × 1Day × 365 days came back exactly 1,000 rows covering first 4 symbols alphabetically — 5 returned data . Surfaced tradebot-mini production cycle requested full-year multi-symbol book “200-period MAs” unknowingly made decisions symbols zero bars . methods now route alpaca_paginate(), following cursor completion; per-symbol bars split across page boundary merged back together.

title: alpaca 0.3.0
text: alpaca_paginate() gains sleep argument — seconds pause page requests (synchronous mode ), stay Alpaca’s free/Basic data-tier rate limit 200 requests/min. get_bars() / get_bars_multi() expose (default 0.3) alongside max_pages (default 1000, runaway guard; pass Inf unbounded). alpaca_paginate() warns truncation rather silently stopping. max_pages reached server still reports next_page_token, pages already fetched returned (work discarded) rlang::warn() fires telling caller resume later start raise max_pages. deliberate convention erroring (throw away data) stopping silently (original bug class).

title: alpaca 0.3.0
text: Corrected get_bars_multi() limit documentation. previously claimed limit “max bars per symbol”; fact per-page total across requested symbols. new text spells notes auto-pagination returns every symbol’s full range regardless limit.

title: alpaca 0.2.3
text: AlpacaAccount$get_activities() $get_activities_by_type() now reject page_size > 100 boundary clear R error instead letting Alpaca’s HTTP 422: "tried set page size N, maximum 100" leak . Surfaced downstream production cycle (tradebot-mini) silently passing page_size = 500L Alpaca enforced cap, point obscure vendor 422 debuggable reading server logs. validation also rejects NA, non-scalar, non-numeric page_size clean rlang::abort() — otherwise bare (page_size > 100L) error "missing value TRUE/FALSE needed" NA input, undermining boundary-check . Closes #7.

title: alpaca 0.2.3
text: get_activities() get_activities_by_type() now document Alpaca’s id-cursor pagination model. new @section Pagination block get_activities() walks recipe — pass previous page’s last id back page_token, stop returned page shorter page_size — worked example. get_activities_by_type() cross-references section. Automated pagination (replacing page_size n / max_total) planned follow-release; public API remain backward-compatible.

title: alpaca 0.2.2
text: coerce_cols(dt, cols, fn) deduplicates cols. Previously passing column name twice — e.g. coerce_cols(dt, c("created_at", "created_at"), rfc3339_to_datetime) — re-feed already-coerced POSIXct vector back lubridate::as_datetime(), silently producing year-56,000+ values (POSIXct numerics reinterpreted RFC-3339 strings). Now uses (col unique(cols)), column coerced regardless callers spell input. Mirrors kucoin binance fixes. rfc3339_to_datetime() returning length-1 NA_POSIXct_ -NA input. result flowed parse_timestamp_cols() /coerce_cols() -> data.table::set(), length-1 POSIXct got coerced existing column’s type (character / numeric / logical) rather replacing column POSIXct one. Endpoints whose @return blocks documented POSIXct therefore quietly return character columns every upstream row missing timestamp. Dropped (.na(x)) short-circuit — lubridate::as_datetime() already returns full-length POSIXct vector -NA input. Mirrors binance ms_to_datetime fix caught binance PR #9 review.

title: alpaca 0.2.2
text: New internal coerce_cols(dt, cols, fn) helper R/helpers_parse.R. Generalises existing parse_timestamp_cols parse_date_cols per-column coercion function. specialised helpers now delegate coerce_cols ’s single core implementation. shape contract binance package’s coerce_cols. Internal @noRd.

title: alpaca 0.2.2
text: DESCRIPTION License field changed License: MIT + file LICENSE License: MIT. previous form required LICENSE 2-line DCF stub (YEAR: / COPYRIGHT HOLDER:), file carries full MIT text — GitHub’s licensee detector wants MIT badge. two requirements conflict; dropping + file LICENSE lets R CMD check skip DCF parse LICENSE file leaving GitHub-visible MIT detection intact. Non-CRAN form.

title: alpaca 0.2.1
text: collapse_string_array_fields now NA-safe. scalar NA_character_ input crash helper — grepl(";", NA_character_, fixed = TRUE) returns NA, propagates (NA) crashes (NA). Mixed vectors like c("real", NA) also wrong: paste(c("real", NA), collapse = ";") produced literal string "real;NA", indistinguishable genuine "NA" value. helper now filters NAs joining, returns NA_character_ every element NA, uses na.rm = TRUE defensively separator-collision check. Ported binance package. alpaca_backfill_bars() longer hides failures return attribute. Per-(symbol, timeframe) errors now surfaced rlang::warn() warnings run, final summary warning lists failure count plus affected pairs end. previous attr(combined, "failures") easy miss; return value now just data.table hidden state. Code read attr(result, "failures") capture warnings withCallingHandlers() tryCatch() instead.

title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: Fields carry calendar dates without time component now parsed Date instead left "YYYY-MM-DD" strings: Corporate actions (AlpacaMarketData$get_corporate_actions()): declaration_date, ex_date, record_date, payable_date. Options contracts (AlpacaOptions$get_contracts(), $get_contract()): expiration_date, open_interest_date, close_price_date. get_corporate_actions(): date_type argument now validated client-side. accepted values suffixed forms Alpaca’s docs SDKs use — "declaration_date", "ex_date", "record_date", "payable_date" — short forms roxygen previously documented ("declaration", "ex", …). short forms accepted server, caller relying already sending invalid request. Adds small shared parse_date_cols(dt, cols) helper walks candidate column names parses "YYYY-MM-DD" strings Date place; columns absent dt silently skipped.

title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: RFC-3339 timestamp fields returned API now parsed POSIXct (UTC) instead character. Previously endpoints (bars, trades, quotes) already ; remaining ones . Now covered: Orders (AlpacaTrading$get_orders(), $get_order(), $submit_order(), etc.): created_at, updated_at, submitted_at, filled_at, expired_at, canceled_at, failed_at, replaced_at. Account (AlpacaAccount$get_account()): created_at. Watchlists ($get_watchlists(), $get_watchlist(), etc.): created_at, updated_at. Activities ($get_activities(), $get_activities_by_type()): transaction_time. News (AlpacaMarketData$get_news()): created_at, updated_at. Snapshots ($get_snapshot(), $get_snapshots_multi()): five nested *_timestamp fields. Previously renamed left character — now parsed consistently standalone bar/trade/quote endpoints. Use lubridate::with_tz() view timezone; underlying instant preserved.

what: Calendar and clock return parsed datetimes (breaking)
title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: AlpacaMarketData$get_calendar() $get_clock() longer return character columns dates times. Instead: get_calendar() returns date/settlement_date Date open/close/session_open/session_close POSIXct localised America/New_York. Alpaca’s /v2/calendar reference page state timezone explicitly; ET inferred /v2 US-, market-data FAQ using NY tz canonical bar aggregation, 09:30 making sense ET wall-clock. named tz handles DST automatically (fixed -05:00 wrong half year). See AlpacaMarketData class docs full reasoning. get_clock() returns timestamp/next_open/next_close POSIXct America/New_York. wall-clock instant preserved exactly — display tz normalised. Use lubridate::with_tz() view elsewhere. previous @return documentation also omitted session_open, session_close, settlement_date; now enumerated. class-level AlpacaMarketData docs now carry “Timezones” section spelling ET--inference assumption calendar times (Alpaca state explicitly reference page) flagging /v3 multi-market endpoints need per-market timezone lookup. TODO(v3) marker sits next ALPACA_EXCHANGE_TZ constant R/helpers_parse.R future migration hard miss.

what: Internal: date / time helpers chokepoint (developer note)
title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: R/helpers_parse.R now carries top--file banner listing five date / time helpers (rfc3339_to_datetime, parse_timestamp_cols, parse_date_cols, combine_et_datetime, hhmm_to_hh_mm) --use-guide. rfc3339_to_datetime() roxygen also explains exists thin wrapper around lubridate::as_datetime (NULL / -NA short-circuit, single chokepoint future change parser). Future contributors route RFC-3339 / pure-date parsing helpers rather calling lubridate directly.

what: Data-shape convention: one entity = one row
title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: Every method returns nested API data now follows single guiding policy: identify entity endpoint, return one row per entity. supporting rules: Variable-length array plain strings single entity (conditions, attributes, permissions, symbols, image_urls, …) → collapsed single character column joined ;. Filter dt[grepl("X", col)]. Recover original vector strsplit(col[1], ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]. Variable-length array objects (orderbook levels, watchlist assets, multi-leg orders, …) → exploded long format parent fields replicated position-index column added order matters (level, leg_index, …). Fixed-schema nested object (snapshot bars, account configurations, …) → flattened wide parent_child columns. Empty / null arrays → NA_character_ (list cells). Empty responses → empty data.table, stub rows. separator ; rather , semicolons far less likely appear inside values . fields value legally contain ; (URLs news image fields), value percent-encoded joining; recover URLdecode() per element strsplit().

what: REVERSALS (from earlier branch state, not from a released version)
title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: Trades — get_latest_trade(), get_latest_trades_multi(), get_trades() previously exploded trade-conditions array long format (one row per condition code). now return one row per trade ;-joined conditions character column. column name moved condition (singular, long-form) conditions (plural, collapsed). News — get_news() previously cross-joined symbols × images article, producing cartesian (3 symbols × 6 images = 18 rows per article). now returns one row per article symbols, image_sizes, image_urls ;-joined character columns. column name moved symbol (singular, long-form) symbols (plural, collapsed).

title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: get_assets() / get_asset() — attributes list column; now ;-joined character ("fractional_eh_enabled;has_options;..."). get_latest_quote() / get_latest_quotes_multi() / get_quotes() — conditions list column; now ;-joined character. One quote = one row regardless many condition codes carries. get_snapshot() / get_snapshots_multi() — inner latest_trade_conditions latest_quote_conditions arrays list columns; now ;-joined character. Bar c close prices stay numeric. get_news() — additionally URL-encodes literal ; characters inside image URLs joining (recover via URLdecode() per element).

title: alpaca 0.2.0

Returns long-format data.table side ("bid" / "ask"), level, price, size, timestamp columns. get_account() (AlpacaAccount) — nested admin_configurations user_configurations objects now flattened wide parent_child columns (e.g. admin_configurations_max_options_trading_level) instead carried list columns.

title: alpaca 0.2.0
text: add_order(), get_order(), get_orders(), get_order_by_client_id(), modify_order() — multi-leg orders (bracket / oco / oto) now return flat data.table one row per order, parent legs equally: leg_index (integer) — NA parent row, 1, 2, ... leg submission order. parent_order_id (character) — NA parent row, parent’s id leg row. Useful filters: dt[.na(parent_order_id)] “just parent orders”, dt[parent_order_id == "<id>"] “legs one specific bracket”. Simple orders return one row helper columns NA.

NULL-defaulted, named-argument calls unaffected. four methods (get_bars, get_bars_multi, get_trades, get_quotes) new params (asof, currency) appended end signature preserve positional ordering. care taken get_option_chain (updated_since now last), modify_order (notional moved last), get_activities (category moved last) callers using positional arguments don’t break. general rule, named arguments safer idiom R — code calls wrapper methods positionally fragile future addition.

title: alpaca 0.1.0
text: Added support new optional parameters Alpaca introduced upstream. additions NULL-defaulted, existing calls unaffected. Stock historical data (get_bars, get_bars_multi, get_trades, get_quotes): asof (symbol-rename handling, e.g. FB → META) currency (ISO 4217). Also documented expanded adjustment enum (spin-, comma-combinable) feed enum (sip, iex, otc, boats). Stock latest data (get_latest_bar, get_latest_trade, get_latest_quote, get_snapshot, _multi variants): currency. Expanded feed enum include delayed_sip overnight. Market calendar (get_calendar): date_type ("TRADING" | "SETTLEMENT"). Assets (get_assets): attributes filter (ptp_no_exception, ptp_with_exception, ipo, has_options, options_late_close, fractional_eh_enabled, overnight_tradable, overnight_halted). Options contracts (get_contracts): show_deliverables, ppind (Penny Program Indicator). Options historical data (get_option_bars, get_option_trades): sort. Options snapshots (get_option_snapshots): updated_since, limit, page_token. (get_option_chain also gains updated_since.) Orders (AlpacaTrading$add_order): legs advanced_instructions. order_class enum now accepts "mleg" multi-leg option strategies. symbol side optional order_class = "mleg" (legs carry info). Order replace (modify_order): notional (IPO orders) advanced_instructions. List orders (get_orders): asset_class, before_order_id, after_order_id. Account configuration (modify_account_config): ptp_no_exception_entry, disable_overnight_trading. max_options_trading_level doc now lists full enum 0–3. Account activities (get_activities): category ("trade_activity" | "non_trade_activity", mutually exclusive activity_types).
